creator: aaron@lo-res.org
text:
in KDE 2.2 the kcheckpass command will not accept a users password.
This can be quite annoying when (un)intenionally locking the screen.

Fix: 

chmod +s /usr/local/bin/kcheckpasswd   :)))
How-To-Repeat: install KDE 2.2 port and log in (via kdm) as normal user. (non root ) lock the screen and try to login -gain -> it will fail.
